mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in INDPRO reflects expanding manufacturing output, lifting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when INDPRO increased month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, further increases in the rate raise the cost of capital and reduce the present value of future corporate cash flows, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: SPX posts positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or the signal's Sharpe ratio exceeds 0.0 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE signal a deteriorating labor market that crimps consumption and compresses profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term's t-statistic falls below 1.5 in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL annualized inflation exceeds 3%, month-over-month increases in RSAFS signal overheating demand that triggers anticipatory monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: In months with CPIAUCSL annualized inflation above 3%, rising RSAFS coincides with positive 5-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or conditional Rank IC is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in HOUST signals durable demand strength and construction sector optimism, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over a two-month span, labor market slack triggers downward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Episodes of UNRATE rising > 0.2pp over 2 months are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or walk-forward accuracy is below 52% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The product of the month-over-month change in INDPRO and the level of FEDFUNDS is negatively related to S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days, as tighter financing conditions amplify the equity risk premium during industrial slowdowns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and INDPRO is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression, or the joint signal fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of fitted months.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising INDPRO and falling RSAFS signals supply outpacing demand, leading to inventory accumulation and margin compression that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Periods where INDPRO rises month-over-month while RSAFS falls are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or the joint signal's Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year window.
